methods.description: Process for selecting and classifying study ponds based on environmental conditions and landscape surroundings to compare chironomid communities.

steps.description: Twenty study ponds were selected for sampling chironomids from among farm ponds in the eastern part of the Banshu Plain.
steps.searchable_summary: select 20 study ponds from Banshu Plain for chironomid sampling
inputs: list of farm ponds in Banshu Plain

steps.description: Of these ponds, 17 ponds were ultimately included in this study, while the other three ponds were omitted due to the poor collection of chironomids.
steps.searchable_summary: filter ponds based on chironomid collection success

steps.description: Their landscape surroundings were regarded as six arable, six forest, and five urban ponds.
steps.searchable_summary: classify ponds based on landscape surroundings

steps.description: They could be separated into two types: excavated and embanked.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize ponds into excavated and embanked types
